extraction.one_line_summary: GREIPEL André won a headwind sprint after GROENWEGEN Dylan jumped 300m out too early, exposing inexperience versus veterans.
tactical_patterns: Strong headwinds final 10km shattered all lead-outs, leaving sprint as pure positional free-for-all favoring experienced opportunists.
ALAPHILIPPE caught in crosswind split day after massive ITT effort, required Quick-Step chase to rejoin—fatigue signals exploitable.
BARDET Romain held KOM jersey from stage 1 through flat stages, signaling breakaway persistence creates jersey control even without mountains.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: caught out in crosswind split one day post-ITT, suggesting deep fatigue from time trial effort made him vulnerable
rider_signals.GROENWEGEN Dylan: negative: jumped 300m out in headwind sprint, fundamental tactical error revealing inexperience in adverse wind conditions
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: positive: positioned perfectly in headwind chaos, used GROENWEGEN as leadout then passed with bike length margin
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: caught too far back to compete despite being top-tier sprinter, poor positioning in disrupted finale
